title: Eco Food-Safe Wood Block Pyramid
summary: 100 small 2x2 blocks in five graduated sizes, stacked into a pyramid, with a fitted plywood storage tray.
description:
This is a counting-and-stacking toy, and the whole project is really about volume: five sizes of block, dozens of each, cut from the same handful of 2x2 boards. Sand the boards before you cut them into blocks, not after — it's dramatically faster to run a sander down one long board than to individually sand a hundred small cubes, and the edges come out more consistent too.

Because these are meant to go in a small child's hands and mouth, finish matters more than usual: use a food-safe oil or leave the wood raw and unfinished rather than reaching for a standard wood finish. The storage tray is sized after the fact, not before — stack the finished blocks, measure the actual outside footprint of the stack, and cut the tray 1/8" larger than that so the blocks drop in and out easily.

tools.note: A stop block is worth setting up — there are over 100 short, repeated cuts.
Sand the full-length boards before cutting into blocks — far faster than sanding each block individually.

materials.name: Food-safe finishing oil
materials.note: Or leave the blocks raw and unfinished — this is a toy young kids will mouth.

cutList.note: Cut 1/8" larger than the stacked blocks' actual outside dimensions — measure your own stack, don't trust a fixed number.

steps.title: Sand the boards, then cut the blocks
steps.body: Sand all five 2x2 boards well before cutting anything — it's far faster and more consistent to sand a full-length board than a hundred individual little cubes afterward. Then cut the blocks to the five sizes in the cut list. Setting up a stop block on your miter saw pays for itself many times over on a cut list this repetitive.

steps.title: Stack and measure for the tray
steps.body: Stack all the blocks into the finished pyramid and measure the outside footprint of the stack — this is what actually determines your tray size, not a fixed number from a plan.

steps.title: Build the tray to fit
steps.body: Cut the plywood tray bottom about 1/8" larger than the stack's measured outside dimensions, so the blocks drop in and lift out easily. Cut the 1x2 frame pieces to fit around it, mitering the corners, and attach.

steps.title: Finish food-safe
steps.body: Apply a food-safe finishing oil to every block and the tray, or leave the wood raw — either is appropriate for a toy a young child will handle and mouth.

images.alt: Stacking wood block pyramid toy with graduated block sizes and a fitted storage tray
